rationale: Keep: the citing paper explicitly says it "extends prior experiential creation research" focused on motives like competence/autonomy, and the cited paper’s core contribution is exactly identifying competence/autonomy drivers and conditions for enjoyment in constrained creative experiences. Given this strong construct-level overlap (experiential creation mechanisms) and the direct DOI match (10.1509/jmkr.44.3.357), this is plausibly a foundational/theory source rather than an incidental mention.
